zotero.abstractNote: Unsupervised disentanglement learning aims to separate the independent factors of variation in data without access to ground-truth labels. Recent theoretical results show that this is impossible without inductive biases on both the models and the data. In this paper, we investigate the setting where a small number of labeled data is available. We show that even a few labeled examples can be used to identify the factors of variation and significantly improve disentanglement performance. We propose a simple yet effective semi-supervised approach that leverages these labels to learn a disentangled representation. We empirically demonstrate that our method outperforms state-of-the-art unsupervised approaches on several datasets and that it is robust to the choice of the few labeled examples.
